title: silene dioica var. glandulosa
intro: Silene dioica var. glandulosa, also known as wood campion or glandular catchfly, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Silene dioica var. glandulosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Silene dioica var. glandulosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
